Combat sport events in 2023
ONE Friday Fights: Nong-O vs. Ramazanov
The poster for ONE Friday Fights: Nong-O vs. Ramazanov
PromotionONE Championship
DateJanuary 20, 2023 (2023-01-20)
VenueLumpinee Boxing Stadium
CityBangkok, Thailand
Event chronology
ONE Fight Night 6: Superbon vs. Allazov ONE Friday Fights: Nong-O vs. Ramazanov ONE Friday Fights: Sangmanee vs. Kulabdam 2

ONE Friday Fights: Nong-O vs. Ramazanov (also known as ONE Lumpinee 1) was a combat sport event produced by ONE Championship that took place on January 20, 2023, at Lumpinee Boxing Stadium in Bangkok, Thailand.[1][2][3]

Background

[edit]

The event marked the promotion's first venue in Lumpinee Boxing Stadium which is a standard Muay Thai stadium in Thailand.[1] In this the ONE Lumpinee event will be broadcast live on Channel 7 HD in Friday at 20:30PM (UTC+07:00).[4]

A ONE Bantamweight Muay Thai World Championship bout between current champion Nong-O Gaiyanghadao and former ONE Bantamweight Kickboxing Champion Alaverdi Ramazanov was originally to take place at ONE on Prime Video 6 but the bout announced moved to headline this event.[5][1] The pairing was previously scheduled at ONE: X, but Ramazanov pull out from the card due to the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine, all Russian athletes were removed from the card as a result of the Singaporean government banning them from entering the country.[6][7]

At the weigh-ins, Saensiri Petch Por.Tor.Or, Mavlud Tupiev and Keivan Soleimani all failed hydration test and were forced to take catchweights. Saensiri weighed in at 151 pounds, 6 pounds over the limit, Tupiev came in at 147 pounds, 2 pounds over the bantamweight limit, Soleimani weighed in at 177 pounds, 7 pounds over the lightweight limit. Both bouts proceeded at catchweight. Saensiri, Tupiev and Soleimani was fined their purse, which went to their opponent Khomarwut F.A.Group, Muangthai P.K.Saenchai and Josh Hill.[8]

Results

[edit]
Main card (YouTube / Watch ONE)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Bantamweight Muay Thai Nong-O Gaiyanghadao (c) def. Alaverdi Ramazanov KO (punches) 3 2:14 [a]
Strawweight Muay Thai Prajanchai P.K.Saenchai def. Kompetch Sitsarawatsuer Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Atomweight Muay Thai Khunsueklek Boomdeksian def. Petchbanrai Singha Mawynn Decision (unanimous) 3 3:00
Catchweight (151 lbs) Muay Thai Khomarwut F.A.Group def. Saensiri Petch Por.Tor.Or Decision (unanimous) 3 3:00 [b]
Flyweight Muay Thai Sonrak Fairtex def. Tai Sor.Jor. Piek-U-Thai Decision (majority) 3 3:00
Catchweight (112 lbs) Muay Thai Sakaengam Jitmuangnon def. Suayai Chor.Hapayak KO (punch) 1 2:47
Preliminary card (FanDuel TV)
Catchweight (140 lbs) Muay Thai Saeksan Or. Kwanmuang def. Tyson Harrison Decision (split) 3 3:00
Lightweight Richard Godoy def. Alexy Lyapunov Decision (split) 3 5:00
Catchweight (147 lbs) Muay Thai Muangthai P.K.Saenchai def. Mavlud Tupiev Decision (unanimous) 3 3:00 [c]
Catchweight (177 lbs) Muay Thai Josh Hill (kickboxer) def. Keivan Soleimani Decision (unanimous) 3 3:00 [d]
Flyweight Colton Kielbasa def. Akihiro Fujisawa Submission (rear-naked choke) 1 0:57
Flyweight Muay Thai Muhammed Sadeghi def. Angelos Giakoumis Decision (unanimous) 3 3:00
  1. ^ For the ONE Bantamweight Muay Thai World Championship.
  2. ^ Saensiri missed weight (151 lbs). Originally scorecard as a unanimous draw (28–28, 28–28, 28–28) but the organization doesn't draw and Khomarwut won due to Saensiri knockdown in Round 1.
  3. ^ Tupiev missed weight (147 lbs).
  4. ^ Soleimani missed weight (177 lbs).

[9]

Bonus awards

[edit]

The following fighters were awarded bonuses.[10]

  • Performance of the Night ($50,000): Nong-O Gaiyanghadao and Saeksan Or. Kwanmuang
  • Performance of the Night ($10,000): Sakaengam Jitmuangnon and Muangthai P.K.Saenchai
